varieties.name: Hass
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, pear-shaped; dark green rough pebbled skin that turns purplish-black when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale green, creamy, rich, nutty flavor; high oil content (18-20%)
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent consistent bearer; long harvest season (Aug-Dec in Queensland)

varieties.name: Reed
varieties.traits: Large, round-shaped fruit; smooth green skin; thin skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent nutty, creamy flavor; considered one of best-tasting; firm texture; large seed
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer; late season variety (Aug-Dec in Queensland); upright growth habit
varieties.availability: Available at specialist Queensland nurseries (Daley's Fruit Tree, Nurseries Online Australia)

varieties.name: Wurtz (Little Cado)
varieties.traits: Dwarf/semi-dwarf tree (2.5-3m); weeping habit; green pear-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent flavor; smooth texture; good oil content; rich and creamy
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent heavy crops; June-August harvest in Queensland
varieties.availability: Widely available in Queensland nurseries as dwarf variety

varieties.name: Lamb Hass
varieties.origin: California, USA; seedling of Gwen variety
varieties.traits: Larger and rounder than Hass; skin darkens when ripe; pebbly texture
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale green with nutty, creamy taste; similar to Hass but larger fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Biennial bearing tendency; Jan-Dec harvest possible; smaller tree than Hass (about 5m)

varieties.name: Pinkerton
varieties.traits: Elongated pear shape with long neck; green, slightly pebbled skin; medium-thick, easy to peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth, creamy texture; nutty, rich flavor; high oil content; small seed (high flesh-to-seed ratio)
varieties.yield_notes: Long harvest period; early to late season; June-August in Queensland; excellent productivity

varieties.name: Gwen
varieties.traits: Slightly larger than Hass; plump, oval shape; thick pebbly green skin that peels easily
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-green creamy flesh; rich, nuanced flavor; high quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer; compact tree suitable for smaller spaces

varieties.name: Jala (Giant)
varieties.origin: Sunshine Coast, Queensland, 2024 (new Australian release from seedling)
varieties.traits: Giant variety - 4x size of Hass (1-1.2kg average); deep green skin; medium glossy appearance; easy to peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale creamy-yellow; smooth, firm texture; rich buttery, nutty taste (similar to Hass/Shepard); good flesh-to-seed ratio; slow to oxidize (stays fresh 5 days after cutting)
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive for large-fruiting type; yields well; June-August harvest; self-pollinating tendencies; fruit in 2-3 years

varieties.name: Secondo
varieties.origin: Queensland selection (Hass × Sharwill cross); Daley's Fruit Tree selection
varieties.traits: Pear-shaped; green skin textured like Hass; vigorous growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth, creamy texture; excellent superior flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Commences fruiting in second year; self-pollinating; July-December harvest

varieties.name: Rincon
varieties.traits: Smallest dwarf variety (2.5m); pear-shaped; smooth green skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild flavor, similar to Hass but less rich
varieties.yield_notes: Early fruiting (2-3 years); small-medium fruit; July-September harvest
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries specializing in dwarf varieties

varieties.name: Sharwill/Sharwil
varieties.origin: Mt. Tamborine, Queensland, Australia, 1954 (Frank Sharpe property seedling; named by James Wilson combining Sharpe + Wilson)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, narrow pear-shaped (8-13cm length); rough, bumpy green skin that stays green when ripe; easy to peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy light green flesh; high oil content (20%+); rich, nutty, buttery flavor with meaty quality; small round seed (13-15% of fruit); 74% flesh content
varieties.yield_notes: Regular, moderate bearer; June-August harvest in Queensland; fruits harvested late in season become creamier

varieties.name: Shepard
varieties.origin: Australia (exclusive to Australia - 2nd most popular variety in country)
varieties.traits: Small-medium sized; smooth green skin that stays green when ripe; smaller tree with spreading habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden buttery flesh; nutty flavor; firm texture that doesn't get mushy; slow to oxidize/brown when cut (excellent for salads)
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer; February-April harvest (early season); grows to 10m

varieties.name: Fuerte
varieties.traits: Small-medium, pear-shaped; thin green skin that stays green when ripe; large spreading tree (8m × 12m)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark green flesh; smooth, creamy texture; mild, slightly sweet nutty flavor; high quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer in warmer climates; tends to produce fruit every second year; June-October; can take 6 years to fruit

varieties.name: Bacon
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, pear-shaped; smooth thin green skin turning purplish-black when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light, subtle, mild flavor; creamy texture; pale green
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive; one of fastest to bear fruit (2-3 years, sometimes 4-5); March-May harvest

varieties.name: Edranol
varieties.traits: Large fruit; rough textured dark skin with bright green bumps; upright growth habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buttery yellow creamy flesh; delicious nutty, buttery flavor; high oil content; easy to peel
varieties.yield_notes: Good consistent producer; July-October harvest
varieties.availability: Available at Australian nurseries (Nurseries Online Australia)

varieties.name: Ettinger
varieties.traits: Thin, smooth green skin; pale green skin; larger than most avocado types
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light green flesh; mild, delicate flavor; smooth, creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer; often grown as 10-15% pollinizer for Hass/Lamb Hass (increases production 20-30%)

varieties.name: Nabal
varieties.origin: Antigua, Guatemala, introduced to USA by F.W. Popenoe in 1927; introduced to Florida 1937
varieties.traits: Medium-large to very large (16-30 oz); round/oval shape; medium-thick bumpy, woody, leathery dark green skin that stays green when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow-green flesh with dark green ring below skin; dense, smooth, creamy, buttery consistency; rich nutty flavor with smoky undertone; 10-14% oil content; large seed
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous tall open tree; can produce very large crops when it fruits; June-October harvest; biennial bearing tendency

varieties.name: Zutano
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit; yellowish-green thin skin; upright tree; pear-shaped
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale green; light texture; slightly lemon-like flavor; lower oil content
varieties.yield_notes: September season opener; ripens November-January

varieties.name: Arka Supreme
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR Bengaluru, India, 2020 (indigenous high-yielding variety developed through seedling selection)
varieties.traits: Oblong shape; attractive appearance; firm texture; spreading growth habit; Type A
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality; 20% fat content (among richest varieties); 7.8°Brix TSS
varieties.yield_notes: ONE OF MOST PRODUCTIVE for Indian conditions: 175-200 kg per mature tree annually; regular bearer

varieties.name: Arka Coorg Ravi
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR Chettalli, Kodagu, Karnataka, India, 2022 (indigenous variety; named after IIHR scientist H. Ravi; 20 years research)
varieties.traits: Shiny dark green appearance giving fresh premium look; Type B
varieties.flesh_fruit: OUTSTANDING pulp recovery >80% (far exceeds most varieties); rich, nutty flavor; creamy consistency; small seed relative to flesh
varieties.yield_notes: HIGH-YIELDING: 100-180 kg per plant (versus 60-80kg traditional varieties); significantly higher than conventional avocados

varieties.name: TKD-1
varieties.origin: HRS Thayangudusai, Tamil Nadu, India (indigenous Indian release)
varieties.traits: Indian-developed variety; pear-shaped to egg-shaped; vibrant green hue; thick dark skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fleshy pulp; minimal water content; rich in healthy fats, vitamins, minerals; subtle unique taste; remarkably smooth texture
varieties.yield_notes: Begins flowering and fruiting around 3-4 years; moderate cultivation effort

varieties.name: Purple / Purple Hybrid
varieties.origin: Introduced to India from Ceylon (Sri Lanka) in 1941; Peradeniya Purple Hybrid also introduced early 1900s
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit; reddish-purple skin when mature; skin ripens from purple to maroon; becomes less glossy when mature; seed coat turns yellowish-white to dark brown at maturity
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich, full taste; good flavor profile; contains less oil than some varieties but excellent taste
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer; July harvest in Sikkim; June-October harvest peak in India

varieties.name: Green / Green Type
varieties.origin: Introduced to India from Ceylon (Sri Lanka) in 1941
varieties.traits: Elongated shape; vibrant green skin; smooth, buttery texture; greenish-yellow when mature
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild taste; creamy, soft flesh; less oil content than Purple but full of taste
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer; September-October harvest in Sikkim; generally harvested June-October in India

varieties.name: Pollock
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1901 (H.S. Pollock property, Miami); West Indian race
varieties.traits: VERY LARGE fruit up to 5 lbs (2.3 kg); oblong to pear-shaped; smooth, glossy bright green skin; upright vigorous tree (30 feet)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, smooth, fine texture; deep yellow changing to yellowish-green near skin; superior quality; 3-5% oil content; large seed frequently loose in cavity
varieties.yield_notes: Shy bearing; July-August harvest; Type B

varieties.name: Fuerte
varieties.traits: Small-medium, pear-shaped; thin green skin; Type B
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth, creamy texture; mild flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer; productive

varieties.name: Hass
varieties.traits: Medium, pear-shaped; dark pebbly skin; Type A
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy, rich, high oil content
varieties.yield_notes: Good consistent bearer

varieties.name: Pinkerton
varieties.traits: Elongated pear shape; green pebbled skin; Type A
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth, creamy; nutty, rich flavor; small seed
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer

varieties.name: Ettinger
varieties.traits: Smooth green skin; Type B
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild, delicate flavor; creamy
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer

varieties.name: Reed
varieties.traits: Large, round; smooth green skin; Type A
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent nutty, creamy flavor; firm texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer

varieties.name: Lamb Hass
varieties.traits: Larger than Hass; pebbly skin; Type A
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale green; nutty, creamy
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer

varieties.name: Booth 7
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1920-1927 (Guatemalan-West Indian hybrid; Will Booth, Krome property); propagated commercially 1935
varieties.traits: Spheroid-obovate shape with rounded apex; small-medium size (10-20 oz, 200-350g); bright green, slightly pebbled, glossy, thick woody skin; Type B
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality; thick flesh; creamy; dark yellow pulp; great taste; 7-13% oil content; medium seed tight in cavity
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer; June/July-October harvest; matures late August-December

varieties.name: Booth 8
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1920-1927 (Guatemalan-West Indian hybrid; Will Booth); propagated commercially 1935
varieties.traits: Oblong fruit; 1-1.5 lbs weight; Type B; grows to 30 ft height × 20 ft diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality flesh
varieties.yield_notes: High producer; matures late August-December; planted extensively 1970s South Florida

varieties.name: Waldin
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1917; West Indian cultivar developed commercially
varieties.traits: West Indian race variety; smooth, leathery, glossy skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality; low oil content (3-10%)
varieties.yield_notes: Sets fruit well when planted alone (self-pollinating tendency); used as rootstock in Florida for uniformity and vigor

varieties.name: Trapp
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1901; West Indian cultivar developed commercially
varieties.traits: West Indian race; smooth skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer

varieties.name: Peradeniya Purple Hybrid
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka (Peradeniya); introduced to India early-mid 1900s
varieties.traits: Purple-tinged variety; hybrid selection
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable bearer

varieties.name: Round
varieties.traits: Round shape (as name suggests)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer

varieties.name: Long / Green Long Shape
varieties.traits: Elongated shape; vibrant green; smooth appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth, buttery texture; mild taste
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer
